deploy.releaseVersionScheme: date (release/YYYY-MM-DD, suffix -2/-3 for multiple per day) while pre-launch/unversioned
deploy.backMerge: after every ship, back-merge main -> develop via a chore/sync-main-into-develop-<date> PR merged with a MERGE COMMIT, so main stays an ancestor of develop. develop<->main movement uses merge commits; feature/refactor->develop use --squash.

build.notes: How many build loops run on a single machine. Default 1 (keeps token spend predictable). To run more, add an extra autodev-build scheduled task per instance, each with a distinct WORKER_SUFFIX (-2, -3, …), its own DEV_PORT, and a staggered cron minute. You can also just add more machines. There is no upper limit beyond your token budget and the machine's resources.

claim.notes: The build loop claims a ready-for-agent work item atomically so no two build loops ever build the same one — coordination is by WORKER id (hostname + optional suffix), NOT by machine, so several loops can share one machine: relabel ready->in-progress, post `🤖 autodev-claim worker=<WORKER>`, wait ~12s, re-read all claim comments; WINNER = earliest git-host server createdAt (ties by lexical worker id). Loser deletes its own claim comment and tries the next item. A stale-claim reaper relabels back to ready-for-agent any in-progress item whose newest claim is older than claimTtlHours with no open PR. The self-recovery guard keys on the worker id, so loops on the same machine don't block each other. This REPLACES any global one-build-at-a-time lock. See the git-host adapter's 'Claiming work' section.

release.notes: autodev-release runs on the PRIMARY machine ONLY (owns prod monitoring + the approval gate). Secondary machines run ONLY autodev-build.

cloudflare._comment: Only present when stack.preset is the Cloudflare preset; delete otherwise. See presets/stack-cloudflare.md.
